data.title: Primary Health Properties reports FY results
data.description:
• Primary Health Properties Plc press release (OTCPK:PHPRF): FY GAAP EPS of 3.10 p.
  • Revenue of £153.6M (+2.9% Y/Y).

data.title: Pacific Basin Shipping GAAP EPS of $0.199, revenue of $2.58B
data.description:
• Pacific Basin Shipping press release (OTCPK:PCFBF): FY GAAP EPS of $2.47.
  • Revenue of $2.58B (+12.2% Y/Y).
  • In view of the continued share discount to the market value of our assets, the Board has approved a new share buyback program of up to $40 million in 2025.
  • Global dry bulk net fleet growth remained steady at 3% in 2024.
  • Debt free on a net basis, with available committed liquidity of $547.6M.

data.title: Sopra Steria Group SA reports FY results
data.description:
• Sopra Steria Group SA press release (OTCPK:SPPSY): FY revenue of €5.78B.
  • Net profit attributable to the group from continuing operations increased 68.4% to €309.3 million.

data.title: Autodesk, Elastic, DoubleVerify in focus amid flurry of earnings from enterprise software
data.description:
• Enterprise software stocks were in focus on Friday amid a flurry of earnings from the sector, including from industry heavyweights such as Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK), Elastic (ESTC) and DoubleVerify (NYSE:DV).
  • Autodesk shares rose 1.5% in premarket trading, as the construction software firm reported fourth-quarter results that topped estimates and said it was laying off 9% of its workforce, amid pressure from an activist investor. Autodesk also said that Mary T. McDowell would step down from its board of directors.
  • Elastic shares jumped 12.5% after the enterprise search company reported third-quarter results that beat Wall Street's estimates and issued a stronger-than-expected forecast for the coming quarter. Following the results, investment firm Scotiabank raised its price target on Elastic to $140 from $127.
  • Expensify (EXFY) jumped 15% after the expense software firm reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter results and issued an outlook for 2025.
  • SoundHound AI (NASDAQ:SOUN) shares rose 2.5% in premarket trading after the voice assistant software company reported fourth-quarter results that topped estimates and upped its outlook for 2025.
  • Alkami Technology (OTC:ALKM) shares were little changed after the financial software firm reported results that were mostly in-line with estimates.
  • On the flip side, DoubleVerify tumbled 20% as its fourth-quarter results and outlook for fiscal 2025 fell short of Wall Street's expectations.
  • Palantir (PLTR) shares fell 5.5% in premarket trading, continuing their string of losses, after the company named Jeffrey Buckley as its Chief Accounting Officer.
  • Jamf Holdings (JAMF) shares fell 4.5% after the company reported fourth-quarter results.

data.title: Grupo Simec, S.A.B. de C.V. reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Grupo Simec, S.A.B. de C.V. press release (NYSE:SIM): Q4 net income of Ps. 2,888 million in the fourth quarter of 2024 compared to Ps. 3,152 million of net income in the third quarter of 2024.
  • Revenue of Ps. 8.83B (+1.0% Y/Y).

data.title: PLDT reports FY results
data.description:
• PLDT press release (NYSE:PHI): Consolidated gross service revenue rose 3% to ₱208.4B in 2024.
  • Earnings Per Share from Continuing Operations Attributable to Common Equity Holders of PLDT ₱149.26.

data.title: Stock index futures rise as inflation report comes as expected
data.description:
Stock index futures climbed on Friday as the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge rose in line with estimates.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) were unchanged, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.5%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.25%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 4.06%.

January Core PCE Price Index came in at +0.3% M/M vs. +0.3% expected and +0.2% prior, and +2.6% Y/Y vs. +2.6% consensus and +2.9% (revised from +2.8%).

PCE Price Index, which includes food and energy prices, was +0.3% M/M vs. +0.4% consensus and +0.3% prior, and +2.5% Y/Y vs. +2.5% consensus and +2.6% prior.

Wall Street caught a bout of heavy selling in the final hour of trading on Thursday, as sentiment took a hit from a post-earnings fall in semiconductor giant Nvidia (NVDA) and more tariff-related moves by U.S. President Donald Trump.

"Markets were in a stressed mood of their own yesterday, with renewed tariff threats from Trump in addition to a sharp tech sell-off that saw the Magnificent 7 post its worst day of 2025," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

"Equity markets reacted to social media posts from US President Trump. Trump is often more coherent on social media than in off-the-cuff comments, and these remarks made clear an intention to hike taxes—US buyers will need 25% more cash to buy Canada or Mexico, and another 10% cash to buy from China," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

On top of the tariffs, we got some more inflationary news from the latest Q4 GDP data, as the Federal Reserve’s preferred inflation measure of PCE was revised up on the second estimate, Reid added.

Wholesale inventories rose in January.

The February Chicago PMI is expected to come in at 40.5.

data.title: Retail inventories fall 0.1% in January
data.description:
• January Retail Inventories (Advance): -0.1% M/M to $821.3B vs. -0.3% prior.
  • On a Y/Y basis, retail inventories rose 4.9%, the U.S. Census Bureau said.

data.title: Fed's preferred inflation gauge rises 2.6% Y/Y in January, in line with estimates
data.description:
January Core PCE Price Index: +0.3% M/M vs. +0.3% expected and +0.2% prior, according to data issued by the U.S. Commerce Department on Friday.

+2.6% Y/Y vs. +2.6% consensus and +2.9% (revised from +2.8%).

PCE Price Index, which includes food and energy prices: +0.3% M/M vs. +0.4% consensus and +0.3% prior.

+2.5% Y/Y vs. +2.5% consensus and +2.6% prior.

Personal income: +0.9% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.4% prior.

Personal Consumption Expenditures: -0.2% M/M vs. +0.2% consensus and +0.6% in December (revised from +0.7%).

Breaking news... check back for updates.
